cooked:
there are many many zoe bodies, they are not all grouped together. there are some things in the game that seem to be strangely un-changable, like the car outside of your house. what is the deal with that damn car! no matter what i change the body entry to, its still the sam. always! mutter mutter kill mutter mutter car mutter mutter

anyway, yeah. you can make zoe move better by changing the animations, they are there, but there wont be one of say, zoe throwing darts, zoe running etc as they are not used in the game. lba1 would be better, because of the fighting zoe clone. your problem is almost certainly that you havnt found ALL of the zoe bodies and replaced them, or that you forgot to build the file (i do that alot:D)

my advice if you want to do a full mod, is that it is tedious. and boring. and sometimes you will go crazy because packed wont do quite the thing you want it to. this is why i gave up on Its a Clones Life, at least until the kinks in SceneManager are worked out (or Scene Editor, depending on who gets the life scripts worked out in a working version first :D). Then I shall come back wreaking a great vengance on my enemies, raining fire an such.

btw download and try out LBArchitect. you can alter and design new maps! the iscometric bricks are tricky at first, buut once you get the hang of it, things really can look very cool
raw:
there are many many zoe bodies, they are not all grouped together. there are some things in the game that seem to be strangely un-changable, like the car outside of your house. what is the deal with that damn car! no matter what i change the body entry to, its still the sam. always! mutter mutter kill mutter mutter car mutter mutter

anyway, yeah. you can make zoe move better by changing the animations, they are there, but there wont be one of say, zoe throwing darts, zoe running etc as they are not used in the game. lba1 would be better, because of the fighting zoe clone. your problem is almost certainly that you havnt found ALL of the zoe bodies and replaced them, or that you forgot to build the file (i do that alot:D)

my advice if you want to do a full mod, is that it is tedious. and boring. and sometimes you will go crazy because packed wont do quite the thing you want it to. this is why i gave up on Its a Clones Life, at least until the kinks in SceneManager are worked out (or Scene Editor, depending on who gets the life scripts worked out in a working version first :D). Then I shall come back wreaking a great vengance on my enemies, raining fire an such.

btw download and try out LBArchitect. you can alter and design new maps! the iscometric bricks are tricky at first, buut once you get the hang of it, things really can look very cool

cooked: yeah, i just checked mine and the problem is that the lba2 anim.hqr, being giganormous, has not been fully documented. therefore packEd cannot tell you which is which. i suggest that if you really want to do this mod you do it with lba1 which has fully listed anims and just about everything else, and all the maps can be modded
raw: yeah, i just checked mine and the problem is that the lba2 anim.hqr, being giganormous, has not been fully documented. therefore packEd cannot tell you which is which. i suggest that if you really want to do this mod you do it with lba1 which has fully listed anims and just about everything else, and all the maps can be modded
